items.title: Nvidia Earnings Preview: The AI Bellwether Faces Its Moment of Truth
items.summary: Nvidia is set to report fiscal Q1 earnings on Wednesday after market close, with consensus expectations for $78.8B revenue (80% YoY growth) and $1.75 EPS (116% YoY growth). The results will be closely watched as they could set the tone for the entire tech sector. Key focus areas include forward guidance, Blackwell chip ramp, gross margins, and China revenue. Options markets are pricing in a potential 7% stock swing. A beat with strong guidance could reignite the AI rally, while any softness could trigger a rotation out of tech.

items.title: Earnings Become Key as Markets Look Past High Oil and Interest Rates
items.summary: Markets are rebounding despite high oil prices and interest rates, with focus shifting to earnings and AI spending forecasts. NVIDIA's earnings report and CEO Jensen Huang's outlook are critical for market direction. Oil prices fell 4% following reports of ships successfully transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions. Growth and momentum stocks are leading, with the trend remaining positive contingent on NVIDIA's guidance and resolution of geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Stock Market Today, May 20: Nvidia Climbs Ahead of Earnings as Investors Await AI Guidance
items.summary: Major stock indices rose on May 20, 2026, as easing Treasury yields and softer oil prices fueled a market rebound. Nvidia climbed ahead of its after-hours earnings report, with expectations for strong guidance on Q2 revenue and gross margins. The broader market sentiment was supported by AI sector strength, with semiconductor and software stocks advancing.

items.title: Nvidia Options Traders Bet On A Big Move As Earnings Loom
items.summary: Nvidia is set to report earnings with options traders positioning for significant volatility. Consensus estimates call for $1.76 EPS on $78.8B revenue, but investors will focus on forward guidance and the company's role in AI infrastructure. The options market is pricing in a ~5.5% post-earnings move, with bullish positioning dominating as call demand significantly outpaces put demand.

items.title: The AI Stock That Could Quietly Double Your Retirement Savings Over the Next Decade
items.summary: Nvidia is positioned as a generational investment opportunity that could double retirement savings over the next decade. The chipmaker benefits from exponential computing demand driven by agentic AI, which requires 1,000 times more tokens per task than standard chatbot queries. With a $5.3 trillion market cap, 73% year-over-year revenue growth, and dominant market share in AI processors, Nvidia maintains substantial pricing power despite competition. The company's continued R&D investments in next-generation chips like Vera Rubin position it to remain the dominant supplier of AI infrastructure as tech giants accelerate capital expenditures.

items.title: Alibaba Takes Aim At Nvidia With New 3X Powerful AI Chip And Next-Gen LLM Model As China Tech War Heats Up
items.summary: Alibaba unveiled its Zhenwu M890 AI chip, delivering triple the performance of its predecessor with 144 GB GPU memory and 800 GB/s interchip bandwidth. The company has shipped 560,000 Zhenwu units to over 400 customers and announced its next-generation Qwen3.7-Max AI model. Alibaba's AI infrastructure investments aim to generate $4.42 billion in recurring revenue by year-end, challenging Nvidia's dominance in China's domestic AI processor market amid U.S. export restrictions.

items.title: 1 Unstoppable Stock to Buy Before It Joins Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, and Microsoft in the $3 Trillion Club
items.summary: Micron Technology is positioned to join the $3 trillion market cap club as AI adoption drives surging demand for memory and storage chips. The company reported record revenue of $23.9 billion in Q2 FY2026 with 196% year-over-year growth and projects Q3 revenue of $33.5 billion. With a current market cap of $784 billion and trading at an attractive 12x forward earnings, Micron could potentially reach $3 trillion by 2030 if it achieves Wall Street's projected 41% annual growth.

items.title: Nvidia's Earnings Are Hours Away. Here Are 3 Things to Watch.
items.summary: Nvidia reports fiscal Q1 earnings with three key items to watch: management's guidance for Q2 (critical indicator of AI demand momentum), progress on the Vera Rubin next-generation chip platform, and any updates on China market opportunities following recent export restriction easing. The stock trades at a P/E of 45, up 19% year-to-date, with hyperscaler capex spending continuing to accelerate.

items.title: The AI Stock That Could Turn a $10,000 Investment Into a Retirement-Changing Sum by 2030
items.summary: Silicon Motion Technology, a smaller AI stock with an $8 billion market cap, has nearly tripled year-to-date and crushed earnings expectations with 105% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026. The company produces NAND flash controllers critical to AI infrastructure and projects 20% sequential growth in Q2, positioning it as a potential high-growth investment opportunity by 2030.

items.title: Pomerantz Law Firm Announces the Filing of a Class Action Against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. and Certain Officers – BW
items.summary: Pomerantz LLP filed a class action lawsuit against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises (BW) and certain officers for alleged securities fraud. The lawsuit alleges that defendants made materially false statements regarding a $2.4 billion power generation contract with Base Electron, failing to disclose that BW's largest shareholder BRC had undisclosed ties to the counterparty and that Applied Digital may not have needed the products/services. The stock fell 11.59% following a Wolfpack Research report exposing these relationships on March 12, 2026.

items.title: Nasdaq Composite Has a Rough Morning; Dow Holds Up Better (Barely)
items.summary: The Nasdaq fell 0.86% on Tuesday as rising Treasury yields (30-year at 5.19%, highest since 2008) and geopolitical tensions weighed on markets. Tech giants Alphabet and Amazon led declines despite positive news, with Alphabet announcing a $5 billion AI infrastructure joint venture with Blackstone. The author advises long-term investors to stay calm, characterizing the volatility as market noise rather than fundamental deterioration.

items.title: SaintQuant Launches Institutional-Grade AI Trading Platform as Goldman Sachs Declares AI the New "Defensive Trade"
items.summary: Goldman Sachs has identified AI as Wall Street's new defensive trade amid inflation and economic uncertainty, with hyperscalers projected to spend $755 billion on capex in 2026. SaintQuant launches a no-code AI trading platform to democratize algorithmic trading for retail investors, offering a $99 free trial and $7 bonus to participate in the institutional AI investment trend.

items.title: AMD Takes A Piece, Amazon Doubles Down: The New Marvell Mania
items.summary: Amazon increased its stake in Marvell Technology by purchasing 170,924 shares in Q1, bringing its total position to 396,352 shares worth $39.26 million. This move follows AMD's recent $6.5 million Marvell investment, signaling that major AI players are taking financial exposure to companies supporting their custom silicon and AI infrastructure buildout. Marvell, a key supplier and co-designer for Amazon's Trainium chips, is increasingly viewed as a levered AI infrastructure play tied to hyperscaler capital expenditure.

items.title: Trump Ally Ron DeSantis Calls Out Big Tech's Hypocrisy On AI Layoffs And 'Cheap Labor' H-1B Visas: 'Not Hard To See Why...'
items.summary: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis criticized major tech companies for contradictory practices: automating white-collar jobs through AI while simultaneously advocating for H-1B visas to hire cheaper foreign labor. DeSantis, aligned with the Trump administration, has taken steps to restrict H-1B usage in Florida public universities. Federal data shows H-1B visa applications at major tech firms have sharply declined in late 2025 amid stricter immigration rules and rising layoffs.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings on May 20: What History Tells Us About Nvidia Stock's Post-Earnings-Release Moves
items.summary: Nvidia is scheduled to report Q1 fiscal 2027 earnings on May 20, 2026. The company has beaten Wall Street earnings estimates in 21 of the past 23 quarters (91.3%), with guidance suggesting 77-80% revenue growth driven by massive capex increases from hyperscalers. However, historical data shows no significant correlation between earnings beat magnitude and immediate stock price movement, suggesting long-term fundamentals matter more than quarterly surprises.

items.title: SpaceX Is Kicking Off IPO Mania -- but It's Also Reviving Stock-Split Euphoria
items.summary: SpaceX is accelerating its IPO to June 12, 2026, aiming to raise $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation and become the eighth-largest U.S. public company. The company will conduct a 5-for-1 forward stock split to improve retail investor accessibility. However, the article warns that despite historical stock-split outperformance, mega-IPOs typically struggle post-debut, and SpaceX's expected astronomical price-to-sales ratio may signal a bubble.

items.title: What's Going On With Blackstone Stock Tuesday?
items.summary: Blackstone announced a joint venture with Google to build AI infrastructure and cloud computing services powered by Google Cloud's TPUs. Blackstone will commit $5 billion in initial equity, with plans to bring 500 megawatts of capacity online in 2027. The partnership aims to meet growing demand for accelerated computing in AI development. BX stock traded up 0.82% in premarket trading, though technical analysis shows bearish signals with the stock trading below key moving averages.

items.title: NextEra Energy's $67 Billion Dominion Acquisition Will Make It the Dominant Power Player in the AI Era and a Must-Own Energy Stock
items.summary: NextEra Energy has agreed to acquire Dominion Energy in a $67 billion all-stock deal, creating the world's largest regulated electric utility. The combined company will serve over 10 million customers across four fast-growing southeastern states and be positioned to capitalize on surging AI data center power demand, with expectations for accelerated earnings growth exceeding 9% annually through 2032.

items.title: Could Cerebras Be the Next Nvidia?
items.summary: Cerebras Systems surged 68% on its IPO debut, raising $5.5 billion as the year's largest IPO, with claims its AI chip outperforms Nvidia's GPUs by up to 15 times in inference speed. However, the company faces significant challenges including heavy customer concentration (62% revenue from one customer), limited customer base compared to Nvidia's decades of market dominance, and historical data suggesting new IPO stocks underperform in the long term.

items.title: This Small Crypto Miner Pivoting to AI Infrastructure Is Up by 75% This Year. Is It a Buy?
items.summary: Keel Infrastructure, a former crypto miner pivoting to AI infrastructure, has gained 75% year-to-date and is approaching a $2.5 billion market cap. With a 2.2 gigawatt capacity pipeline and plans to sign three major leases in 2026, the company is positioned to generate significant recurring revenue from tech giants, though it still lacks major deals compared to competitors like Cipher Mining and Iren.

items.title: UPS Could Thrive in a Post-Amazon World
items.summary: UPS is executing a strategic pivot to reduce Amazon deliveries by 50% and shift toward higher-margin customers, including small and medium-sized businesses and healthcare products. Despite near-term headwinds from trade tensions and macro weakness, the company is showing incremental improvements with rising SMB volumes and record healthcare revenue. With a forward P/E of 14x and 6.6% dividend yield, UPS presents a buying opportunity as its turnaround gains momentum.

items.title: Eli Lilly Stock: Next Stop $2,000?
items.summary: Eli Lilly's stock has surged above $1,000 following strong Q1 results driven by its dominance in weight-loss and diabetes markets. Despite increasing competition from rivals like Novo Nordisk and Amgen, the company maintains advantages through superior pipeline candidates like Retatrutide and diversified revenue streams in oncology, immunology, and neuroscience. AI-powered drug development efforts could further accelerate growth, potentially enabling the stock to double within six years.

items.title: WaterBridge Stock Is Up 55% Since IPO. One Fund Bought Up $12 Million More Last Quarter
items.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management increased its stake in WaterBridge Infrastructure by 504,627 shares worth $12.02 million in Q1 2026. WaterBridge shares have surged 55% since their September IPO, now trading at $31.06. The water management company reported strong Q1 results with $201 million in revenue and 51% adjusted EBITDA margins, while raising full-year guidance. The investment reflects confidence in the infrastructure play supporting oil and gas producers across major U.S. shale basins.

items.title: Why S&P Global Stock Topped the Market Today
items.summary: S&P Global's stock surged 3.54% as the company announced $2 billion in senior notes financing for its Mobility Global holding company ahead of the planned spinoff of its automotive data unit (Global Mobility/Carfax) to shareholders. The financing, combined with a $500 million credit facility, provided investors confidence in the company's financial strength.

items.title: SoftVest and Blackbeard Holdings Announce Proposed Business Combination with Permian Basin Royalty Trust
items.summary: SoftVest, a significant unitholder of Permian Basin Royalty Trust (PBT), and Blackbeard Holdings announced a proposed business combination to create a new NYSE-listed company. The transaction would convert PBT's net profits interests into a cost-free 15% royalty interest, add approximately 66,500 acres of surface estate, and leverage Blackbeard's proven operational track record. Former PBT unitholders would own ~58% of the new entity while Blackbeard and affiliates would own ~42%. The deal requires unitholder approval and regulatory clearance.

items.title: Nvidia Doubled Down on Its Second-Largest AI Holding. Should Investors Follow Suit?
items.summary: Nvidia nearly doubled its stake in CoreWeave to 47.2 million shares (11% ownership) worth $4.9 billion in Q1 2026, signaling strong confidence in the neocloud provider. CoreWeave reported 112% year-over-year revenue growth to $2 billion and a $99.4 billion backlog, though the company remains unprofitable. The article suggests CoreWeave could be worth a small position in a balanced portfolio given Nvidia's mentorship and accelerating growth, though it trades at 8x sales compared to Nvidia's 27x forward earnings.

items.title: Elon Musk Loses OpenAI Lawsuit-What The Trial Revealed About AI Investing
items.summary: A federal jury dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, ruling that the case was filed too late under statute of limitations. The verdict clears major legal obstacles for OpenAI's anticipated IPO and secures Microsoft's $228.3 billion stake in the company. The trial revealed OpenAI's 35x forward revenue multiple and $24 billion annualized revenue run rate, positioning it for a potential $1 trillion public listing.

items.title: Is Now the Time to Buy Forgotten FAANG Stock Netflix?
items.summary: Netflix, once a FAANG darling, has maintained steady growth with 12.6% annualized revenue growth over five years and 16% revenue growth in 2025. The company continues to raise prices while retaining customers, is investing in AI to reduce production costs, and achieved an 83% year-over-year net income increase in Q1 2026 (boosted by a $2.8B merger termination fee). Despite being edged out of the Magnificent Seven, Netflix's combination of top-line growth, margin expansion, and strategic AI investments suggest potential upside at its current 28 P/E ratio.

items.title: Anthropic Buys Stainless, Acquiring Startup Behind OpenAI's Developer Libraries
items.summary: Anthropic has acquired Stainless, a developer tools startup founded in 2022, for approximately $300 million to improve developer experience and enhance connections between AI agents and external systems. Stainless will wind down its products including its SDK generator. The acquisition comes as Anthropic reportedly seeks new funding at an $800 billion valuation and has seen its annualized revenue reach $30 billion in April, surpassing OpenAI's $24 billion run rate.

items.title: AI Boom Resembles the Internet Rally as Markets Party Like 1999
items.summary: The article compares the current AI boom to the 1999 internet rally, highlighting strong growth prospects for AI and data center stocks. Nvidia is expected to deliver impressive quarterly results with 79.7% sales growth and 119.8% earnings growth. The author recommends staying fully invested to capitalize on this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity, with data center order backlogs expected to sustain momentum through 2027. A potential Samsung labor strike could worsen memory chip shortages.

items.title: Rivian's Robotics Company Is Now Worth More Than $3 Billion. Investors Could Benefit in 2 Important Ways.
items.summary: Rivian's robotics spinoff, Mind Robotics, has reached a $3.4 billion valuation after raising $400 million in its latest funding round. The company plans to use Mind's robots to improve manufacturing efficiency and reduce production costs, while also commercializing the technology to other industrial companies. Rivian owns approximately 38% of Mind Robotics, positioning investors to benefit from potential future valuations increases or an IPO in the growing industrial robotics market.

items.title: Meta’s Selloff Looks Mispriced Against Its AI-Driven Earnings Power
items.summary: Meta's stock fell 8% post-Q1 earnings despite delivering 33% revenue growth, 62% EPS growth, and strong AI-driven monetization metrics including a $20B value optimization suite and 1.6% conversion rate improvements. The article argues the selloff is mispriced, as Meta trades at 19x forward P/E (14% below 5-year average) and 11x forward operating cash flow versus Alphabet's 22x, despite growing 50% faster. The capex increase to $125-145B is attributed to component costs rather than new projects, and the AI infrastructure is directly generating measurable returns unlike the metaverse spending era.

items.title: Dan Loeb Dumps Microsoft, Slashes Nvidia And Rail Stocks In Sweeping Q1 Portfolio Overhaul
items.summary: Hedge fund billionaire Daniel Loeb's Third Point LLC significantly reshuffled its portfolio in Q1 2026, completely exiting positions in Microsoft, Chipotle, Constellation Energy, Alibaba, Spotify, and Thermo Fisher Scientific. The fund also dramatically reduced stakes in Nvidia (from 2.95M to 190K shares), Amazon, Taiwan Semiconductor, and railroad stocks Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern.

items.title: Warren Buffett’s Cash Hoard: Why $397 Billion Sits on the Sidelines
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's cash hoard has reached a record $397.4 billion under new CEO Greg Abel, growing from $373 billion left by Buffett at year-end 2025. The massive cash position reflects disciplined value investing amid historically expensive market valuations, with Berkshire selling $172.93 billion in equities since 2022 while earning 4-5% on Treasury bills. However, this conservative stance cost shareholders approximately $125 billion in foregone gains compared to S&P 500 returns over 2023-2025, particularly missing mega-cap growth stocks like Nvidia, Microsoft, and Meta.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Could Decide Whether the AI Trade Still Holds
items.summary: Markets faced mixed signals as geopolitical tensions eased on Iran sanctions reports, causing oil prices to reverse sharply lower. Bond yields surged globally on inflation concerns, with the 10-year Treasury hitting 4.631%. Nvidia's Wednesday earnings are positioned as the most critical event for the AI capital expenditure cycle, with options pricing a potential 7% move. Key corporate actions include NextEra-Dominion merger announcement, Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio restructuring under new CEO Abel, and Regeneron's failed Phase 3 trial.

items.title: Prediction: This Beaten-Down Tech Stock Will Outperform the S&P 500 by 2027
items.summary: Reddit stock has declined over 30% year-to-date despite strong fundamentals including 17% year-over-year growth in daily active users (126.8M), 69% revenue growth, and a 30.8% net profit margin in Q1 2026. The article argues concerns about AI search engines cannibalizing Reddit traffic are overblown, as the platform's unique user-generated content and AI licensing deals position it well for future growth.

items.title: Alphabet's New Growth Engine Is Something Investors Can't Miss
items.summary: Alphabet has begun directly selling its Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) to select clients, significantly boosting Google Cloud's growth. In Q1, Google Cloud revenue surged 63% year-over-year with operating margins improving from 19% to 33%. This positions Alphabet as a major player in AI hardware and cloud computing, with the company's overall Q1 revenue and operating income growing 22% and 30% respectively.

items.title: Kettering University Names Alumnus Qasar Younis, Co-founder and CEO of Applied Intuition, as 2026 Commencement Speaker
items.summary: Kettering University has selected Qasar Younis, co-founder and CEO of Applied Intuition, as the 2026 Commencement speaker and will award him an honorary Doctor of Engineering degree. Younis, a 2004 alumnus, leads a prominent AI company focused on physical AI for autonomous systems across automotive, defense, and industrial sectors. The university highlighted how Younis's career exemplifies Kettering's engineering education model combined with professional industry experience.

items.title: Mistral AI CEO Warns Europe Has Two Years To Avoid Becoming US 'Vassal State:' Here's Why
items.summary: Mistral AI CEO Arthur Mensch warned during a French National Assembly hearing that Europe has only two years to build independent AI infrastructure before becoming dependent on U.S. tech giants. He highlighted that the battle for AI dominance is fundamentally about controlling chips, energy, and compute resources, with U.S. firms deploying approximately $1 trillion next year. Mensch also cited Europe's fragmented regulations and shallow capital markets as critical barriers to competing at scale.

items.title: What Tim Cook's Trip to China Could Mean for Apple Investors
items.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook participated in a Trump administration delegation to Beijing, a significant trip for the company given its heavy reliance on Chinese manufacturing and the Chinese market. Easing tariffs and improved U.S.-China relations could benefit Apple by securing manufacturing relationships, reducing costs, and maintaining market access. Chinese President Xi Jinping signaled China will 'open wider' for business, and with Apple's stock at all-time highs above $300, investors should be cautiously optimistic about the trip's outcomes.

items.title: Better Buy: Micron or Nvidia Stock
items.summary: Nvidia and Micron are both thriving on AI-driven data center demand, but they present different investment profiles. Nvidia dominates GPU markets with sustainable competitive advantages and 79-86% projected revenue growth, making it a stable long-term AI play. Micron benefits from memory chip shortages with explosive 261-246% growth projections, but faces cyclical risks as supply catches up, potentially returning to single-digit P/E ratios. The choice depends on investor risk tolerance: Nvidia for set-and-forget stability, Micron for higher upside with volatility.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Drop Following Drone Strike On UAE Nuclear Plant—Regeneron Pharma, NextNRG, ServiceNow In Focus
items.summary: U.S. stock futures fell on Monday following Friday's sharp declines, triggered by a drone strike near the UAE's Barakah nuclear power plant. While UAE officials confirmed no radiation leak or injuries, geopolitical tensions remain elevated. Key movers include Regeneron Pharmaceuticals tumbling 11.92% after failed Phase 3 trials, NextNRG surging 91.16% on strong quarterly revenue growth, and ServiceNow rising 3.50% following a partnership announcement with Experian. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 declined in premarket trading.

items.title: Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Just Sold 16 Stocks, but Piled Into an AI Titan That's Now a Top-5 Position for Berkshire Hathaway
items.summary: Greg Abel, Warren Buffett's successor as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, exited 16 stock positions in Q1 2026 citing expensive market valuations. However, he significantly increased Berkshire's stake in Alphabet to a top-5 holding, more than tripling the position to approximately $23 billion. The move reflects Abel's focus on value investing and Alphabet's strong fundamentals including its search monopoly and rapidly growing Google Cloud business with AI integration.

items.title: PropTech Market Size Expected to Reach USD 165 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
items.summary: The PropTech market is experiencing rapid expansion, valued at $43 billion in 2025 and projected to reach $165 billion by 2035 with a 13.9% CAGR. Growth is driven by AI-powered property management solutions, smart building technologies, cloud computing, and blockchain-based transactions. The U.S. market alone is expected to grow from $11 billion to $42.3 billion over the same period. Integrated platforms dominate with 68% market share, while cloud deployment and residential properties are expected to show the fastest growth rates.

items.title: 3 Quantum Computing Stocks to Buy Right Now
items.summary: Quantum computing is positioned as the next major technology trend after AI. The article recommends three stocks: D-Wave Quantum for its specialized quantum annealing approach and early commercial success, IonQ for holding the world record in quantum computing accuracy and strong revenue growth, and Nvidia for its hybrid computing strategy and supporting software platforms that will bridge quantum and traditional computing.

items.title: This AI Stock Has Every Quality Warren Buffett Looks for in a Long-Term Hold
items.summary: Microsoft is highlighted as an AI stock that embodies Warren Buffett's investment philosophy, featuring a strong competitive moat through its enterprise software dominance, reliable cash flow generation, and a 21-year track record of dividend increases. Despite a rough start to 2026, the company remains an attractive long-term investment for those following Buffett's principles.

items.title: Is Micron the Next Nvidia?
items.summary: Micron Technology is experiencing strong AI-driven growth with Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue up 196% YoY and gross margins exceeding 70%, trading at a forward P/E of 7.4 versus the semiconductor industry median of 30x. However, while Micron is a critical supplier of high-bandwidth memory for AI accelerators, it lacks Nvidia's platform dominance and pricing power. Unlike Nvidia, which controls the AI ecosystem architecture, Micron is a commodity supplier vulnerable to price competition and cyclical margin compression as supply capacity increases.

items.title: Nvidia Stock Is Poised to Rocket From the Booming Space Economy
items.summary: Nvidia is positioned to dominate AI applications in the rapidly growing space economy, which is projected to reach $1.8 trillion by 2035. The company has launched space-hardened AI computing platforms and is partnering with space companies like Planet Labs and Starcloud. With first-mover advantage in space AI development and strong fundamentals, Nvidia is expected to generate significant revenue from space applications alongside its terrestrial AI business.

items.title: The AI Stock With a 10-Year Head Start That Wall Street Still Hasn't Fully Priced In
items.summary: Alphabet has a significant competitive advantage in AI infrastructure through its custom tensor processing units (TPUs), developed over a decade ago. The company's eighth-generation TPUs now offer specialized variants for training and inference, enabling lower-cost AI operations. Alphabet is beginning to sell these chips directly through partners, creating a new revenue stream. With a forward P/E of 28, the stock may still have upside potential as the market hasn't fully valued its integrated AI capabilities.

items.title: Veterans For American Update: CLJA Veterans Pool Issues (Mo’ Money More Problems)
items.summary: A Colorado bankruptcy judge foreclosed what may be the largest Chapter 11 case in state history involving 440,040 veteran members of the Camp Lejeune Justice Act Settlement after a critical court filing missed the May 1 deadline. The case is being reopened following recovery of the misdirected package. VetWork leadership is pursuing statutory rights to reopen the case and plans a major CLJA veterans awareness conference in Denver this summer.

items.title: Why Everyone Is Talking About Lumentum Stock -- and Why Investors Should Care
items.summary: As AI systems scale, optical networking is emerging as a critical infrastructure layer. Lumentum, which develops optical and photonic technologies for data transmission, is positioned to benefit from this shift. The company reported 90% YoY revenue growth to $808M in Q3 FY2026 and guided for $1B in Q4 revenue. While AI infrastructure stocks have already rallied significantly, optical networking may become foundational to future AI data centers, potentially creating long-term opportunities.

items.title: Great News: This Magnificent 7 Stock May Still Have Lots of Room to Run
items.summary: Alphabet is capitalizing on AI demand across Google Cloud, Gemini, Search, and enterprise tools, with strong quarterly momentum. However, the company's massive capital expenditure plans raise concerns about whether it can sustain earnings growth without compromising free cash flow.

items.title: SpaceX Accelerates Its IPO Timeline: 10 Things You Need to Know
items.summary: SpaceX is accelerating its IPO timeline with plans to debut on June 12, 2026, on the Nasdaq under ticker symbol SPCX. The company aims to raise up to $75 billion, making it 2.5 times larger than Saudi Aramco's previous record IPO. SpaceX approved a 5-for-1 stock split and will benefit from Nasdaq's new fast-entry rule allowing inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 after just 15 trading sessions. However, historical precedent suggests large IPOs often struggle in their first six months post-debut.

items.title: Consumer Tech News (May 11-15): Trump–Xi Talk Fails to Deliver Chip Deal, Elon Musk vs. OpenAI Trail & More
items.summary: Global semiconductor stocks retreated after Trump and Xi's summit failed to announce major chip agreements or breakthroughs on NVIDIA's China business. Key developments include Taiwan Semiconductor's $31.28B capital spending approval for AI chip manufacturing, Meta offering free WhatsApp access to competing AI developers to resolve EU antitrust concerns, Trump Mobile delivering delayed $499 smartphones, and the Elon Musk vs. OpenAI trial concluding with closing arguments. Notable earnings included strong results from Applied Materials, Cisco, and Tower Semiconductor, while Alibaba reported mixed results and Birkenstock faced margin pressures.

items.title: Is a SpaceX-Tesla Merger Elon Musk's Endgame? Here Are 4 Factors Investors Need to Consider.
items.summary: Following SpaceX's planned $75 billion IPO that would value the company at ~$2 trillion, rumors suggest Elon Musk may merge SpaceX with Tesla. While such a merger could create synergies across rockets, AI, robotics, and EVs, it would face significant execution risks, regulatory hurdles including antitrust scrutiny, and operational complexity at a critical time when Tesla faces Chinese competition and SpaceX burns $1 billion monthly on xAI scaling. SpaceX's dual-class share structure gives Musk 50% voting control post-IPO, making the merger feasible if pursued.

items.title: 3 Reasons Why Amazon Is a Top AI Investment
items.summary: Amazon is positioned as a top AI investment due to three key factors: its custom AI chips (Trainium series) are experiencing triple-digit revenue growth and replacing GPUs similar to how its Graviton chips replaced Intel CPUs; massive capital expenditures ($200 billion this year) are backed by major client commitments and will generate recurring revenue long-term; and its valuation is reasonable compared to peers when measured by operating cash flow metrics.

items.title: These 2 Nvidia-Backed Stocks Look Like Genius Investments
items.summary: CoreWeave and Nebius, two neocloud providers backed by Nvidia, are experiencing rapid growth in AI computing infrastructure. CoreWeave grew revenue 112% to $2.1B in Q1 with a $100B backlog, while Nebius achieved 684% revenue growth to $399M. Both companies face profitability challenges due to high capital expenditure requirements but show strong long-term potential as AI demand continues.

items.title: Prediction: May 21 Will Be a Huge Day for the Stock Market. Here's What You Need to Know.
items.summary: Nvidia's Q1 FY2027 earnings report on May 20 after market close is expected to significantly impact the stock market on May 21. As the AI chip leader, Nvidia's guidance on demand, inference growth, China export progress, and AI's interaction with other technologies will provide crucial visibility into the broader AI sector and tech stocks. The company forecasts 77% revenue growth to $78 billion with 74% gross margins.
